---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size80[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size70[.center[

Welcome to the prisoner's dilemma (mark 2)

]]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. Reserve the extra room(s)

2. Print

- Game table handout
- Recording sheets handout (if no chalk board for the group)
- Tracking sheet for you
- Final tracking sheet for winning leader, all students listed by group

3. Calculate reward

- Enough so majority of winning group can get 5 or whole group gets fewer

Number in class / 2 = Group size
(Group size / 2) + 1 = Group majority
Group majority * 5 = Game reward

<br>



---

class: middle, slideblue

.size50[.center[**A Competitive Prisoner's Dilemma**]]

<br>

.size40[
+ A single game played across multiple rounds.

+ Each round your group must choose whether to cooperate or defect.

+ Your reward depends on the choice made by the other group.
]

???

Today we replay our prisoners' dilemma game from week four but with a few newly added wrinkles.

Just like last time we have three basic rules:

- A single game played across multiple rounds.

- Each round your group must choose whether to cooperate or defect.

- Your reward depends on the choice made by the other group.

<br>

* split the class in half *

Stand up and move to the opposite sides of the room.



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/09_1-PD_Table2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: bottom

.size50[.center[**A Competitive Prisoner's Dilemma**]]

???

Now, today's PD game has been modified slightly.

SLIDE

First, we are now playing to reach a target.

Each round your team will choose whether to cooperate or defect and the points will be allocated as per this table.

Your goal is to be the first team to reach 300 points.

<br>

EVERYBODY STILL CLEAR ON HOW TO READ THIS GAME BOARD?

The table shows four possible outcomes for each round based on the choice made by each group.

Group 1's results are underlined to be helpful.

ANY QUESTIONS ON THE GAME BOARD?

<br>

The Reward: Bonus points on the final

The winning team will receive X bonus points to distribute in the group.

<br>

There are two additional tweaks to our game.

1. After each round we will have an international negotiation.

Your group will send someone to negotiate with the other team on your behalf.




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/09_1-skipper.jpg')
background-size: 35%
background-position: right

.pull-left[
.size45[.center[**Your Fearless Leader**]]

.size40[
1. Selects your negotiator,

2. Chooses to cooperate or defect each round, and

3. On the winning team, distributes the bonus points.
]]

???

2. Each group is now an explicitly democratic state.

The leader has three responsibilities:

1. Selects a single negotiator to represent your state in negotiations,

2. Makes the ultimate decision each round as to whether to cooperate or defect, and

3. On the winning team, distributes the bonus points to his or her constituents however they choose.

ANY QUESTIONS ON THE POWERS OF THE LEADER?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/09_1-voting.png')
background-size: 93%
background-position: bottom

.size45[.center[**Your Fearless (and TEMPORARY) Leader**]]

???

As I said, you are each a democracy.

Your first job will be to elect a leader using a simple majority rule (50% + 1).

And because you are a democracy, you may replace the leader by a simple vote at any point during the game.

ANY QUESTIONS ON THIS?


---

background-image: url('libs/Images/09_1-PD_Table2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: bottom

.size50[.center[**A Competitive Prisoner's Dilemma**]]

???

ANY QUESTIONS BEFORE WE START PLAYING?

Ok, group A to 307, group B to 313 down the hall.

When you get there pick your leader and get ready for round 1.

<br>

* Negotiate after each round. *

* Play until a winner. *

<br>

ALL back to room 301

* give list of team members to the leader *


---

background-image: url('libs/Images/09_1-patronage.jpg')
background-size: 83%
background-position: center

???

Leader, this list includes everybody in class.

On Monday when we get back I want you to announce how you will be distributing the spoils.

Next week we divy up the prizes and explore the two-level games model of international politics.
